reaction_name: 1,3-Dipolar cycloaddition of in-situ-generated nitrile oxide with phenylacetylene to form ethyl 5-phenylisoxazole-3-carboxylate
bond_formed: C–O and C–C bonds in isoxazole ring
bond_broken: N–O bond rearrangement in ethyl nitroacetate during nitrile oxide generation; loss of H2O

feed_streams.reasoning: All reagents are premixed in DES; phenylacetylene must be present when nitrile oxide forms in situ to trap it and prevent dimerization to furoxan. No incompatibility between phenylacetylene and ethyl nitroacetate at room temperature — reaction only proceeds at elevated temperature.

reagents.name: ethyl nitroacetate
reagents.notes: Generates nitrile oxide in situ at 120°C via dehydration; 2 equiv used to ensure complete conversion of phenylacetylene

reagents.name: tetrabutylammonium bromide (TBAB)
reagents.role: deep eutectic solvent component (hydrogen bond acceptor)
reagents.equiv_or_loading: 1:5 molar ratio with ethylene glycol (1 mL DES total)
reagents.notes: Forms DES with ethylene glycol; acts as reaction medium promoting nitrile oxide generation without added base or catalyst

reagents.name: ethylene glycol (EG)
reagents.role: deep eutectic solvent component (hydrogen bond donor)
reagents.notes: Hydrogen bond donor in DES; high boiling point enables 120°C operation

mechanism_steps.description: Thermal dehydration/tautomerization of ethyl nitroacetate to generate ethoxycarbonylformonitrile oxide (nitrile oxide intermediate) in situ. The DES medium facilitates this transformation without external base.

Concerted [3+2] cycloaddition of the nitrile oxide 1,3-dipole across the C≡C triple bond of phenylacetylene, forming the isoxazole ring with regioselectivity favoring the 3,5-disubstituted product (ethyl 5-phenylisoxazole-3-carboxylate).

stream_logic.reasoning: Single premixed feed: all components are stable together at room temperature. The nitrile oxide only forms at elevated temperature (120°C) in the heated reactor zone. Premixing ensures the dipolarophile (phenylacetylene) is present to immediately trap the transient nitrile oxide, suppressing dimerization to furoxan. DES is viscous (~50-100 cP at RT), so a single stream minimizes mixing challenges. Consider warming the feed reservoir to ~40-50°C to reduce viscosity for pumping.

mixing_order_reasoning: All reagents can be premixed in the DES at room temperature since the reaction only initiates at 120°C. This is critical: the dipolarophile must be homogeneously distributed when nitrile oxide forms to trap it efficiently. Two-stream mixing would be problematic due to the high viscosity of DES causing poor micromixing.
deoxygenation_reasoning: No oxygen-sensitive species; thermal pericyclic mechanism is not affected by O2.
quench_reasoning: Batch protocol quenches with water for workup/extraction purposes only, not to stop a runaway reaction. In flow, the product stream can be collected directly and worked up offline.
wavelength_reasoning: Not a photochemical reaction; purely thermal activation at 120°C.
confidence_notes: High confidence in mechanism and flow translation. Key uncertainties: (1) DES viscosity at room temperature is high (~50-100 cP); pumping may require warming the feed to 40-50°C or using a high-pressure syringe pump. (2) Pressure drop through a narrow-bore coil with viscous DES needs careful calculation via Hagen-Poiseuille; a wider bore (1.0-1.6 mm ID) coil may be needed. (3) The 15 min residence time at 120°C should translate well to flow with a back-pressure regulator (BPR) to keep the system above the boiling point of any volatile components. (4) 2.0 equiv of ethyl nitroacetate is used — the excess and any furoxan byproduct may need separation downstream. (5) Water generated in situ from nitrile oxide formation is miscible with DES and should not cause issues.
